\"users.0.email\" → \"users[0].email\")\n61\t\tnormalizedFieldName := FormatFieldPath(fieldName)\n62\t\n63\t\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n64\t\tif normalizedFieldName == \"(unknown field)\" {\n65\t\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n66\t\t\t\treturn parent\n67\t\t\t}\n68\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n69\t\t}\n70\t\n71\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n72\t\t\t// Normalize parent as well in case it contains array indices\n73\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n74\t\n75\t\t\t// If parent normalizes to unknown field, just return the field name\n76\t\t\tif normalizedParent == \"(unknown field)\" {\n77\t\t\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n78\t\t\t}\n79\t\n80\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedParent, normalizedFieldName)\n81\t\t}\n82\t\n83\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n84\t}\n85\t\n86\t// FormatFieldLocationWith creates a detailed field location string with context.\n87\t// This is useful for error messages that need to show exactly where an error occurred.\n88\t// Unlike FormatFieldLocation in format_helpers.go, this supports parent paths.\n89\t//\n90\t// Parameters:\n91\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n92\t//   - location: Additional location context (e.g., \"line 5\", \"position 123\")\n93\t//   - parent: Optional parent path\n94\t//\n95\t// Returns a formatted location string.\n96\t//\n97\t// Example usage:\n98\t//\n99\t//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"line 5\", \"request\")\n100\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email at line 5\"\n101\t//\n102\t//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"\", \"request\")\n103\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n104\tfunc FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName string, location string, parent string) string {\n105\t\tref := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n106\t\tlocation = strings.TrimSpace(location)\n107\t\n108\t\tif location != \"\" {\n109\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s at %s\", ref, location)\n110\t\t}\n111\t\n112\t\treturn ref\n113\t}\n114\t\n115\t// FormatFieldListWith formats a list of field names into a readable string with conjunction.\n116\t// Unlike FormatFieldList in format_helpers.go, this supports custom conjunctions.\n117\t//\n118\t// Parameters:\n119\t//   - fields: Slice of field names\n120\t//   - conjunction: The conjunction to use (e.g., \"and\", \"or\")\n121\t//\n122\t// Returns a formatted list string.\n123\t//\n124\t// Example usage:\n125\t//\n126\t//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\"}, \"and\")\n127\t//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\n128\t//\n129\t//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\", \"name\"}, \"and\")\n130\t//\t// Returns: \"email, password, and name\"\n131\tfunc FormatFieldListWith(fields []string, conjunction string) string {\n132\t\tif len(fields) == 0 {\n133\t\t\treturn \"\"\n134\t\t}\n135\t\n136\t\tif len(fields) == 1 {\n137\t\t\treturn fields[0]\n138\t\t}\n139\t\n140\t\tif len(fields) == 2 {\n141\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s %s\", fields[0], conjunction, fields[1])\n142\t\t}\n143\t\n144\t\t// Oxford comma style\n145\t\tallButLast := fields[:len(fields)-1]\n146\t\tlast := fields[len(fields)-1]\n147\t\n148\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s, %s %s\", strings.Join(allButLast, \", \"), conjunction, last)\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t// =============================================================================\n152\t// ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n153\t// =============================================================================\n154\t\n155\t// FormatErrorMessage creates a standardized error message from components.\n156\t// This ensures error messages are consistently formatted.\n157\t//\n158\t// Parameters:\n159\t//   - errorType: The type of error (use ErrorType enum or string)\n160\t//   - message: The core error message\n161\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n162\t//\n163\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n164\t//\n165\t// Example usage:\n166\t//\n167\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"required\", \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n168\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n169\t//\n170\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n171\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n172\tfunc FormatErrorMessage(errorType string, message string, fieldName string) string {\n173\t\terrorType = strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n174\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n175\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n176\t\n177\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n178\t\n179\t\t// Add error type in brackets\n180\t\tif errorType != \"\" {\n181\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType))\n182\t\t}\n183\t\n184\t\t// Add field name if present\n185\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n186\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n187\t\t}\n188\t\n189\t\t// Add message\n190\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n191\t\n192\t\treturn builder.String()\n193\t}\n194\t\n195\t// FormatErrorWithValues creates an error message showing expected vs actual values.\n196\t// This is useful for validation errors where values don't match expectations.\n197\t//\n198\t// Parameters:\n199\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n200\t//   - expected: The expected value\n201\t//   - actual: The actual value\n202\t//   - customMessage: Optional custom message prefix\n203\t//\n204\t// Returns a formatted error message with value comparison.\n205\t//\n206\t// Example usage:\n207\t//\n208\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"status_code\", 200, 404, \"\")\n209\t//\t// Returns: \"status_code: expected 200, got 404\"\n210\t//\n211\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"count\", 5, 3, \"Not enough items\")\n212\t//\t// Returns: \"count: Not enough items (expected 5, got 3)\"\n213\tfunc FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, customMessage string) string {\n214\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n215\t\tcustomMessage = strings.TrimSpace(customMessage)\n216\t\n217\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n218\t\n219\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n220\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n221\t\t}\n222\t\n223\t\tif customMessage != \"\" {\n224\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", customMessage))\n225\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"(expected %v, got %v)\", expected, actual))\n226\t\t} else {\n227\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v, got %v\", expected, actual))\n228\t\t}\n229\t\n230\t\treturn builder.String()\n231\t}\n232\t\n233\t// FormatErrorWithRange creates an error message for range validation failures.\n234\t// This is useful for numeric range validations or length validations.\n235\t//\n236\t// Parameters:\n237\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n238\t//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value\n239\t//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value\n240\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n241\t//\n242\t// Returns a formatted range error message.\n243\t//\n244\t// Example usage:\n245\t//\n246\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithRange(\"age\", 0, 120, 150)\n247\t//\t// Returns: \"age: value 150 is outside valid range [0, 120]\"\n248\tfunc FormatErrorWithRange(fieldName string, minValue interface{}, maxValue interface{}, actualValue interface{}) string {\n249\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n250\t\n251\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n252\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n253\t\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n254\t\t}\n255\t\n256\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n257\t\t\tactualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n258\t}\n259\t\n260\t// FormatErrorWithPattern creates an error message for pattern validation failures.\n261\t// This is useful for format validations like email, URL, regex patterns.\n262\t//\n263\t// Parameters:\n264\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n265\t//   - pattern: The pattern that failed (can be a description)\n266\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n267\t//\n268\t// Returns a formatted pattern error message.\n269\t//\n270\t// Example usage:\n271\t//\n272\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithPattern(\"email\", \"must contain @\", \"invalidemail\")\n273\t//\t// Returns: \"email: value 'invalidemail' does not match pattern (must contain @)\"\n274\tfunc FormatErrorWithPattern(fieldName string, pattern string, actualValue interface{}) string {\n275\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n276\t\tpattern = strings.TrimSpace(pattern)\n277\t\n278\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n279\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n280\t\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, pattern)\n281\t\t}\n282\t\n283\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n284\t\t\tactualValue, pattern)\n285\t}\n286\t\n287\t// =============================================================================\n288\t// ERROR TYPE FORMATTING\n289\t// =============================================================================\n290\t\n291\t// FormatErrorType creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorType enum.\n292\t// This converts technical error type names to readable labels.\n293\t//\n294\t// Parameters:\n295\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n296\t//\n297\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n298\t//\n299\t// Example usage:\n300\t//\n301\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeRequired)\n302\t//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n303\t//\n304\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeFormat)\n305\t//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\n306\tfunc FormatErrorType(errorType ErrorType) string {\n307\t\tswitch errorType {\n308\t\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n309\t\t\treturn \"Required Field\"\n310\t\tcase ErrTypeFormat:\n311\t\t\treturn \"Format Error\"\n312\t\tcase ErrTypeRange:\n313\t\t\treturn \"Range Error\"\n314\t\tcase ErrTypeLength:\n315\t\t\treturn \"Length Error\"\n316\t\tcase ErrTypeType:\n317\t\t\treturn \"Type Error\"\n318\t\tcase ErrTypeValue:\n319\t\t\treturn \"Invalid Value\"\n320\t\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n321\t\t\treturn \"Duplicate Value\"\n322\t\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n323\t\t\treturn \"Conflict\"\n324\t\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n325\t\t\treturn \"Unknown Error\"\n326\t\tdefault:\n327\t\t\treturn string(errorType)\n328\t\t}\n329\t}\n330\t\n331\t// FormatErrorTypeFrom creates a user-friendly label from a string error type.\n332\t// This converts technical error type strings to readable labels.\n333\t//\n334\t// Parameters:\n335\t//   - errorTypeStr: The error type string\n336\t//\n337\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n338\t//\n339\t// Example usage:\n340\t//\n341\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"required\")\n342\t//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n343\t//\n344\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"format\")\n345\t//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\n346\tfunc FormatErrorTypeFrom(errorTypeStr string) string {\n347\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n348\t\treturn FormatErrorType(et)\n349\t}\n350\t\n351\t// =============================================================================\n352\t// CATEGORY FORMATTING\n353\t// =============================================================================\n354\t\n355\t// FormatCategory creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorCategory.\n356\t// This converts technical category names to readable labels.\n357\t//\n358\t// Parameters:\n359\t//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n360\t//\n361\t// Returns a formatted category label.\n362\t//\n363\t// Example usage:\n364\t//\n365\t//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryHTTP)\n366\t//\t// Returns: \"HTTP Protocol\"\n367\t//\n368\t//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryValidation)\n369\t//\t// Returns: \"Data Validation\"\n370\tfunc FormatCategory(category ErrorCategory) string {\n371\t\tswitch category {\n372\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n373\t\t\treturn \"HTTP Protocol\"\n374\t\tcase CategoryContent:\n375\t\t\treturn \"Content\"\n376\t\tcase CategoryValidation:\n377\t\t\treturn \"Data Validation\"\n378\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n379\t\t\treturn \"Performance\"\n380\t\tcase CategorySecurity:\n381\t\t\treturn \"Security\"\n382\t\tcase CategoryCustom:\n383\t\t\treturn \"Custom\"\n384\t\tdefault:\n385\t\t\treturn string(category)\n386\t\t}\n387\t}\n388\t\n389\t// =============================================================================\n390\t// SEVERITY FORMATTING\n391\t// =============================================================================\n392\t\n393\t// FormatSeverity creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorSeverity.\n394\t// This converts technical severity names to readable labels.\n395\t//\n396\t// Parameters:\n397\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n398\t//\n399\t// Returns a formatted severity label.\n400\t//\n401\t// Example usage:\n402\t//\n403\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityCritical)\n404\t//\t// Returns: \"CRITICAL\"\n405\t//\n406\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityHigh)\n407\t//\t// Returns: \"High\"\n408\tfunc FormatSeverity(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n409\t\tswitch severity {\n410\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n411\t\t\treturn \"CRITICAL\"\n412\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n413\t\t\treturn \"High\"\n414\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n415\t\t\treturn \"Medium\"\n416\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n417\t\t\treturn \"Low\"\n418\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n419\t\t\treturn \"Info\"\n420\t\tdefault:\n421\t\t\treturn string(severity)\n422\t\t}\n423\t}\n424\t\n425\t// FormatSeverityWithIndicator adds visual indicators to severity labels.\n426\t// This is useful for console output or UI display.\n427\t//\n428\t// Parameters:\n429\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n430\t//\n431\t// Returns a severity label with visual indicator.\n432\t//\n433\t// Example usage:\n434\t//\n435\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityCritical)\n436\t//\t// Returns: \"[!] CRITICAL\"\n437\t//\n438\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityHigh)\n439\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High\"\n440\tfunc FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n441\t\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n442\t\tlabel := FormatSeverity(severity)\n443\t\n444\t\tif indicator != \"\" {\n445\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", indicator, label)\n446\t\t}\n447\t\n448\t\treturn label\n449\t}\n450\t\n451\t// severityIndicator returns a visual indicator for a severity level.\n452\t// Uses emoji indicators for clear visual distinction:\n453\t// - 🚨 (Critical): Alert/ sirens for critical issues\n454\t// - ⚠️ (High): Warning sign for high severity\n455\t// - ⚡ (Medium): Lightning bolt for medium severity\n456\t// - ℹ️ (Low): Info for low severity issues\n457\t// - 💡 (Info): Light bulb for informational messages\n458\t// - ❓ (Unknown): Question mark for unknown severity\n459\tfunc severityIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n460\t\tswitch severity {\n461\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n462\t\t\treturn \"🚨\"\n463\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n464\t\t\treturn \"⚠️\"\n465\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n466\t\t\treturn \"⚡\"\n467\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n468\t\t\treturn \"ℹ️\"\n469\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n470\t\t\treturn \"💡\"\n471\t\tdefault:\n472\t\t\treturn \"❓\"\n473\t\t}\n474\t}\n475\t\n476\t// =============================================================================\n477\t// EXPECTED VS ACTUAL FORMATTING\n478\t// =============================================================================\n479\t\n480\t// FormatExpectedActual formats an ExpectedActual struct into a readable string.\n481\t// This function handles different value types and formats them side-by-side.\n482\t// Returns empty string if the ExpectedActual is empty (both values nil).\n483\t//\n484\t// Parameters:\n485\t//   - ea: The ExpectedActual struct to format\n486\t//\n487\t// Returns a formatted string showing expected vs actual values, or empty string if nil/empty.\n488\t//\n489\t// Example usage:\n490\t//\n491\t//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n492\t//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActual(ea)\n493\t//\t// Returns: \"expected: 200 (OK), actual: 404 (Not Found)\"\n494\t//\n495\t//\tea := NewExpectedActual(\"test@example.com\", \"invalid\")\n496\t//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActual(ea)\n497\t//\t// Returns: \"expected: 'test@example.com', actual: 'invalid'\"\n498\tfunc FormatExpectedActual(ea ExpectedActual) string {\n499\t\tif ea.IsEmpty() {\n500\t\t\treturn \"\"\n501\t\t}\n502\t\n503\t\tvar expectedStr, actualStr string\n504\t\n505\t\t// Format expected value\n506\t\tif ea.HasExpected() {\n507\t\t\tswitch exp := ea.Expected.(type) {\n508\t\t\tcase int:\n509\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", exp, getStatusCodeDescription(exp))\n510\t\t\tcase []int:\n511\t\t\t\tparts := make([]string, len(exp))\n512\t\t\t\tfor i, code := range exp {\n513\t\t\t\t\tparts[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, getStatusCodeDescription(code))\n514\t\t\t\t}\n515\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"one of [%s]\", strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n516\t\t\tcase string:\n517\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", exp)\n518\t\t\tcase float64:\n519\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%.2f\", exp)\n520\t\t\tcase []string:\n521\t\t\t\tquoted := make([]string, len(exp))\n522\t\t\t\tfor i, s := range exp {\n523\t\t\t\t\tquoted[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", s)\n524\t\t\t\t}\n525\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(quoted, \", \"))\n526\t\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n527\t\t\t\texpectedStr = formatMapValue(exp)\n528\t\t\tcase []interface{}:\n529\t\t\t\texpectedStr = formatSliceValue(exp)\n530\t\t\tdefault:\n531\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", exp)\n532\t\t\t}\n533\t\t}\n534\t\n535\t\t// Format actual value\n536\t\tif ea.HasActual() {\n537\t\t\tswitch act := ea.Actual.(type) {\n538\t\t\tcase int:\n539\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", act, getStatusCodeDescription(act))\n540\t\t\tcase string:\n541\t\t\t\t// Truncate long strings\n542\t\t\t\tif len(act) > 100 {\n543\t\t\t\t\tact = act[:100] + \"...\"\n544\t\t\t\t}\n545\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", act)\n546\t\t\tcase float64:\n547\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%.2f\", act)\n548\t\t\tcase []string:\n549\t\t\t\tquoted := make([]string, len(act))\n550\t\t\t\tfor i, s := range act {\n551\t\t\t\t\tquoted[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", s)\n552\t\t\t\t}\n553\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(quoted, \", \"))\n554\t\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n555\t\t\t\tactualStr = formatMapValue(act)\n556\t\t\tcase []interface{}:\n557\t\t\t\tactualStr = formatSliceValue(act)\n558\t\t\tdefault:\n559\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", act)\n560\t\t\t}\n561\t\t}\n562\t\n563\t\t// Build the formatted string\n564\t\tvar parts []string\n565\t\tif expectedStr != \"\" {\n566\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", expectedStr))\n567\t\t}\n568\t\tif actualStr != \"\" {\n569\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %s\", actualStr))\n570\t\t}\n571\t\n572\t\tif len(parts) == 0 {\n573\t\t\treturn \"\"\n574\t\t}\n575\t\n576\t\treturn strings.Join(parts, \", \")\n577\t}\n578\t\n579\t// formatMapValue formats a map value for display in error messages.\n580\tfunc formatMapValue(m map[string]interface{}) string {\n581\t\tif len(m) == 0 {\n582\t\t\treturn \"{}\"\n583\t\t}\n584\t\n585\t\tvar pairs []string\n586\t\tfor k, v := range m {\n587\t\t\tpairs = append(pairs, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %v\", k, v))\n588\t\t}\n589\t\n590\t\t// Limit output for large maps\n591\t\tif len(pairs) > 5 {\n592\t\t\tpairs = pairs[:5]\n593\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s, ...}\", strings.Join(pairs, \", \"))\n594\t\t}\n595\t\n596\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s}\", strings.Join(pairs, \", \"))\n597\t}\n598\t\n599\t// formatSliceValue formats a slice value for display in error messages.\n600\tfunc formatSliceValue(s []interface{}) string {\n601\t\tif len(s) == 0 {\n602\t\t\treturn \"[]\"\n603\t\t}\n604\t\n605\t\tvar items []string\n606\t\tfor _, v := range s {\n607\t\t\titems = append(items, fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", v))\n608\t\t}\n609\t\n610\t\t// Limit output for large slices\n611\t\tif len(items) > 5 {\n612\t\t\titems = items[:5]\n613\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s, ...]\", strings.Join(items, \", \"))\n614\t\t}\n615\t\n616\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(items, \", \"))\n617\t}\n618\t\n619\t// FormatExpectedActualInline formats ExpectedActual values inline (compact format).\n620\t// This is useful for embedding in error messages where space is limited.\n621\t// Returns empty string if the ExpectedActual is empty.\n622\t//\n623\t// Parameters:\n624\t//   - ea: The ExpectedActual struct to format\n625\t//\n626\t// Returns a compact formatted string.\n627\t//\n628\t// Example usage:\n629\t//\n630\t//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n631\t//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActualInline(ea)\n632\t//\t// Returns: \"(expected 200, got 404)\"\n633\tfunc FormatExpectedActualInline(ea ExpectedActual) string {\n634\t\tif ea.IsEmpty() {\n635\t\t\treturn \"\"\n636\t\t}\n637\t\n638\t\tvar parts []string\n639\t\tif ea.HasExpected() {\n640\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v\", ea.Expected))\n641\t\t}\n642\t\tif ea.HasActual() {\n643\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"got %v\", ea.Actual))\n644\t\t}\n645\t\n646\t\tif len(parts) == 0 {\n647\t\t\treturn \"\"\n648\t\t}\n649\t\n650\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"(%s)\", strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n651\t}\n652\t\n653\t// =============================================================================\n654\t// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\n655\t// =============================================================================\n656\t\n657\t// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\n658\t// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\n659\t// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\n660\t//\n661\t// Parameters:\n662\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n663\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n664\t//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n665\t//\n666\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n667\t//\n668\t// Example usage:\n669\t//\n670\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n671\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n672\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n673\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n674\t//\t}\n675\t//\n676\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, nil)\n677\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\n678\t//\n679\t//\tctx := NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"})\n680\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, ctx)\n681\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\"\n682\tfunc FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext) string {\n683\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n684\t\n685\t\t// Add severity if requested\n686\t\tif includeSeverity {\n687\t\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n688\t\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n689\t\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n690\t\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n691\t\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n692\t\t\t} else {\n693\t\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n694\t\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n695\t\t\t}\n696\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n697\t\t}\n698\t\n699\t\t// Add error type and field\n700\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n701\t\n702\t\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n703\t\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n704\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n705\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n706\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n707\t\t\t}\n708\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n709\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n710\t\t\t}\n711\t\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n712\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n713\t\t\t}\n714\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n715\t\t}\n716\t\n717\t\t// Add location if present in the error itself\n718\t\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n719\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n720\t\t}\n721\t\n722\t\t// Add context information if provided and not empty\n723\t\tif context != nil && !context.IsEmpty() {\n724\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n725\t\t\tvar contextParts []string\n726\t\n727\t\t\tif context.HasLocation() {\n728\t\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", context.Location))\n729\t\t\t}\n730\t\n731\t\t\tif context.HasRelatedFields() {\n732\t\t\t\tfieldsList := FormatFieldList(context.RelatedFields)\n733\t\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"related fields: %s\", fieldsList))\n734\t\t\t}\n735\t\n736\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(strings.Join(contextParts, \", \"))\n737\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n738\t\t}\n739\t\n740\t\t// Add suggestions if present\n741\t\tif len(err.Suggestions) > 0 {\n742\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\"\\nSuggestions:\")\n743\t\t\tfor _, suggestion := range err.Suggestions {\n744\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\n  - %s\", suggestion))\n745\t\t\t}\n746\t\t}\n747\t\n748\t\treturn builder.String()\n749\t}\n750\t\n751\t// FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual creates a comprehensive, formatted error message\n752\t// with optional ExpectedActual parameter for value comparison. This extends FormatValidationErrorFull\n753\t// to support structured expected vs actual value display.\n754\t//\n755\t// Parameters:\n756\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n757\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n758\t//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n759\t//   - expectedActual: Optional ExpectedActual for structured value comparison display\n760\t//\n761\t// Returns a formatted error message string with optional expected/actual comparison.\n762\t//\n763\t// The ExpectedActual parameter takes precedence over err.Expected/err.Actual when provided.\n764\t// If expectedActual is nil or empty, the function falls back to err.Expected/err.Actual.\n765\t// If both are empty, no value comparison is included.\n766\t//\n767\t// Example usage:\n768\t//\n769\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n770\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n771\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n772\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n773\t//\t}\n774\t//\n775\t//\t// With ExpectedActual parameter\n776\t//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n777\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err, true, nil, ea)\n778\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (expected: 200 (OK), actual: 404 (Not Found))\"\n779\t//\n780\t//\t// Without ExpectedActual parameter (falls back to err.Expected/err.Actual)\n781\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err, true, nil, ExpectedActual{})\n782\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\n783\tfunc FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext, expectedActual ExpectedActual) value: The value to truncate\n1135\t//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n1136\t//\n1137\t// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\n1138\t//\n1139\t// Example usage:\n1140\t//\n1141\t//\ttruncated := TruncateString(\"very long string here\", 10)\n1142\t//\t// Returns: \"very long...\"\n1143\tfunc TruncateString(value string, maxLength int) string {\n1144\t\tif maxLength < 3 {\n1145\t\t\tmaxLength = 3\n1146\t\t}\n1147\t\n1148\t\tif len(value) <= maxLength {\n1149\t\t\treturn value\n1150\t\t}\n1151\t\n1152\t\treturn value[:maxLength-3] + \"...\"\n1153\t}\n1154\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_5053b009b0e041279ea1fe6e","session_id":"9d2b4270-c2fc-4060-a36a-d992357b20c8","uuid":"808d6495-f230-4c60-b9d7-05e75a227cee","timestamp":"2026-07-15T23:51:29.083Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Find validation error handling"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_bce37d4dc2584dcb88c03975","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tpackage server\n2\t\n3\timport (\n4\t\t\"fmt\"\n5\t\t\"net/http\"\n6\t\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n7\t\t\"strings\"\n8\t\t\"testing\"\n9\t\n10\t\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/validate\"\n11\t)\n12\t\n13\t// =============================================================================\n14\t// HTTP STATUS CODE VALIDATION HELPERS\n15\t// =============================================================================\n16\t// This module provides comprehensive HTTP status code validation utilities\n17\t// for error response testing. It supports single status codes, multiple allowed\n18\t// status codes, pattern-based range validation, and flexible validation patterns.\n19\t//\n20\t// Functions:\n21\t// - ValidateStatusCodeInt: Basic status code validation with expected/actual ints\n22\t// - ValidateStatusCodePattern: Validate status code matches pattern (e.g., '4xx', '5xx')\n23\t// - ValidateStatusCode: Validate single expected status code (test helper)\n24\t// - ValidateStatusCodeAny: Validate against multiple allowed status codes\n25\t// - ValidateStatusCodeRange: Validate status code is within range\n26\t// - CheckStatusCode: Non-asserting version that returns boolean\n27\t// - AssertStatusCode: Flexible validation supporting both assertion and boolean modes\n28\t// - AssertStatusCodeAny: Flexible multi-code validation with assertion mode\n29\t// - StatusCodeMatchResult: Detailed validation result with error context\n30\t// - GetStatusCodeDescription: Get human-readable status code description\n31\t// - parseStatusCodePattern: Parse pattern string to extract century digit\n32\t// =============================================================================\n33\t\n34\t// ValidateStatusCodeInt validates expected vs actual HTTP status codes.\n35\t//\n36\t// This is a basic status code validation function that compares two integers\n37\t// and returns an error if they don't match. It's useful for simple validation\n38\t// scenarios where you have direct access to status code integers.\n39\t//\n40\t// Parameters:\n41\t//   - expected: The expected HTTP status code (e.g., 200, 404, 500)\n42\t//   - actual: The actual HTTP status code received\n43\t//\n44\t// Returns:\n45\t//   - nil if the status codes match\n46\t//   - error with descriptive message if they don't match\n47\t//\n48\t// Example (matching codes):\n49\t//   err := ValidateStatusCodeInt(200, 200)\n50\t//   // err == nil\n51\t//\n52\t// Example (non-matching codes):\n53\t//   err := ValidateStatusCodeInt(200, 404)\n54\t//   // err.Error() == \"status code mismatch: expected 200 (OK), got 404 (Not Found)\"\n55\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeInt(expected, actual int) error {\n56\t\tif expected == actual {\n57\t\t\treturn nil\n58\t\t}\n59\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"status code mismatch: expected %d (%s), got %d (%s)\",\n60\t\t\texpected, GetStatusCodeDescription(expected),\n61\t\t\tactual, GetStatusCodeDescription(actual))\n62\t}\n63\t\n64\t// StatusCodeMatchResult contains detailed information about a status code validation.\n65\t//\n66\t// This type provides comprehensive error context for debugging validation failures,\n67\t// including the expected and actual status codes with their descriptions, the\n68\t// response object context, and a formatted error message.\n69\ttype StatusCodeMatchResult struct {\n70\t\t// Match indicates whether the status code matched successfully\n71\t\tMatch bool\n72\t\n73\t\t// Expected is the status code that was expected\n74\t\tExpected int\n75\t\n76\t\t// Actual is the status code that was received\n77\t\tActual int\n78\t\n79\t\t// ExpectedDescription is the human-readable description of the expected status code\n80\t\tExpectedDescription string\n81\t\n82\t\t// ActualDescription is the human-readable description of the actual status code\n83\t\tActualDescription string\n84\t\n85\t\t// ResponseContext contains information about the response object\n86\t\tResponseContext string\n87\t\n88\t\t// Error is a formatted error message describing the mismatch\n89\t\tError string\n90\t}\n91\t\n92\t// String returns a human-readable representation of the validation result.\n93\tfunc (r StatusCodeMatchResult) String() string {\n94\t\tif r.Match {\n95\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code match: %d (%s)\", r.Actual, r.ActualDescription)\n96\t\t}\n97\t\treturn r.Error\n98\t}\n99\t\n100\t// AssertStatusCode validates status code with flexible assertion mode.\n101\t//\n102\t// This function supports both boolean-only mode (for conditional logic) and\n103\t// assertion mode (with descriptive error messages). When assertMode is true\n104\t// and validation fails, it returns a detailed error message showing expected\n105\t// vs actual values along with response context.\n106\t//\n107\t// Parameters:\n108\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (*httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n109\t//   - expectedCode: The expected HTTP status code\n110\t//   - assertMode: If true, return detailed error message on failure; if false, return bool only\n111\t//\n112\t// Returns:\n113\t//   - StatusCodeMatchResult with detailed validation information\n114\t//\n115\t// Example (boolean mode):\n116\t//   result := AssertStatusCode(w, 200, false)\n117\t//   if !result.Match {\n118\t//       // Handle non-200 case\n119\t//   }\n120\t//\n121\t// Example (assertion mode):\n122\t//   result := AssertStatusCode(w, 404, true)\n123\t//   if !result.Match {\n124\t//       t.Error(result.Error)  // Full error with expected vs actual\n125\t//   }\n126\tfunc AssertStatusCode(response interface{}, expectedCode int, assertMode bool) StatusCodeMatchResult {\n127\t\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n128\t\n129\t\t// Build response context for error messages\n130\t\tresponseContext := getStatusCodeResponseContext(response)\n131\t\n132\t\t// Check if status codes match\n133\t\tmatches := actualCode == expectedCode\n134\t\n135\t\tif matches {\n136\t\t\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n137\t\t\t\tMatch:              true,\n138\t\t\t\tExpected:           expectedCode,\n139\t\t\t\tActual:             actualCode,\n140\t\t\t\tExpectedDescription: GetStatusCodeDescription(expectedCode),\n141\t\t\t\tActualDescription:   GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode),\n142\t\t\t\tResponseContext:     responseContext,\n143\t\t\t\tError:              \"\",\n144\t\t\t}\n145\t\t}\n146\t\n147\t\t// Build detailed error message\n148\t\terrorMsg := buildStatusCodeMismatchError(\n149\t\t\texpectedCode,\n150\t\t\tactualCode,\n151\t\t\tresponseContext,\n152\t\t)\n153\t\n154\t\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n155\t\t\tMatch:              false,\n156\t\t\tExpected:           expectedCode,\n157\t\t\tActual:             actualCode,\n158\t\t\tExpectedDescription: GetStatusCodeDescription(expectedCode),\n159\t\t\tActualDescription:   GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode),\n160\t\t\tResponseContext:     responseContext,\n161\t\t\tError:              errorMsg,\n162\t\t}\n163\t}\n164\t\n165\t// AssertStatusCodeAny validates status code against multiple allowed codes.\n166\t//\n167\t// This function is similar to AssertStatusCode but accepts multiple allowed\n168\t// status codes. Returns detailed error information when validation fails,\n169\t// including the full list of allowed status codes.\n170\t//\n171\t// Parameters:\n172\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n173\t//   - allowedCodes: Slice of acceptable status codes\n174\t//   - assertMode: If true, return detailed error message on failure\n175\t//\n176\t// Returns:\n177\t//   - StatusCodeMatchResult with detailed validation information\n178\t//\n179\t// Example:\n180\t//   result := AssertStatusCodeAny(w, []int{200, 201, 204}, true)\n181\t//   if !result.Match {\n182\t//       t.Error(result.Error)\n183\t//   }\n184\tfunc AssertStatusCodeAny(response interface{}, allowedCodes []int, assertMode bool) StatusCodeMatchResult {\n185\t\tif len(allowedCodes) == 0 {\n186\t\t\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n187\t\t\t\tMatch:              false,\n188\t\t\t\tExpected:           0,\n189\t\t\t\tActual:             0,\n190\t\t\t\tExpectedDescription: \"\",\n191\t\t\t\tActualDescription:   \"\",\n192\t\t\t\tResponseContext:     getStatusCodeResponseContext(response),\n193\t\t\t\tError:              \"AssertStatusCodeAny: allowedCodes cannot be empty\",\n194\t\t\t}\n195\t\t}\n196\t\n197\t\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n198\t\tresponseContext := getStatusCodeResponseContext(response)\n199\t\n200\t\t// Check if actual code matches any in the allowed list\n201\t\tfor _, allowedCode := range allowedCodes {\n202\t\t\tif actualCode == allowedCode {\n203\t\t\t\tallowedDesc := formatCodeList(allowedCodes)\n204\t\t\t\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n205\t\t\t\t\tMatch:              true,\n206\t\t\t\t\tExpected:           actualCode,\n207\t\t\t\t\tActual:             actualCode,\n208\t\t\t\t\tExpectedDescription: allowedDesc,\n209\t\t\t\t\tActualDescription:   GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode),\n210\t\t\t\t\tResponseContext:     responseContext,\n211\t\t\t\t\tError:              \"\",\n212\t\t\t\t}\n213\t\t\t}\n214\t\t}\n215\t\n216\t\t// Build helpful error message showing what was allowed vs. what was received\n217\t\tallowedDesc := formatCodeList(allowedCodes)\n218\t\terrorMsg := buildStatusCodeMismatchErrorAny(\n219\t\t\tallowedDesc,\n220\t\t\tactualCode,\n221\t\t\tresponseContext,\n222\t\t)\n223\t\n224\t\treturn StatusCodeMatchResult{\n225\t\t\tMatch:              false,\n226\t\t\tExpected:           0,\n227\t\t\tActual:             actualCode,\n228\t\t\tExpectedDescription: allowedDesc,\n229\t\t\tActualDescription:   GetStatusCodeDescription(actualCode),\n230\t\t\tResponseContext:     responseContext,\n231\t\t\tError:              errorMsg,\n232\t\t}\n233\t}\n234\t\n235\t// buildStatusCodeMismatchError creates a detailed error message for status code mismatches.\n236\t//\n237\t// This helper builds a comprehensive error message that includes:\n238\t// - The expected status code with description\n239\t// - The actual status code with description\n240\t// - Response object context (type and status if available)\n241\tfunc buildStatusCodeMismatchError(expected, actual int, responseContext string) string {\n242\t\tvar b strings.Builder\n243\t\n244\t\tb.WriteString(\"Status code mismatch:\\n\")\n245\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: %d (%s)\\n\", expected, GetStatusCodeDescription(expected)))\n246\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n\", actual, GetStatusCodeDescription(actual)))\n247\t\n248\t\tif responseContext != \"\" {\n249\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context:  %s\\n\", responseContext))\n250\t\t}\n251\t\n252\t\treturn b.String()\n253\t}\n254\t\n255\t// buildStatusCodeMismatchErrorAny creates a detailed error message for multi-code mismatches.\n256\t//\n257\t// This helper builds a comprehensive error message when validating against multiple\n258\t// allowed status codes, showing all allowed codes vs the actual received code.\n259\tfunc buildStatusCodeMismatchErrorAny(allowedList string, actual int, responseContext string) string {\n260\t\tvar b strings.Builder\n261\t\n262\t\tb.WriteString(\"Status code mismatch:\\n\")\n263\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: one of [%s]\\n\", allowedList))\n264\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n\", actual, GetStatusCodeDescription(actual)))\n265\t\n266\t\tif responseContext != \"\" {\n267\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context:  %s\\n\", responseContext))\n268\t\t}\n269\t\n270\t\treturn b.String()\n271\t}\n272\t\n273\t// getStatusCodeResponseContext extracts contextual information about the response object.\n274\t//\n275\t// This helper builds a string description of the response object for error messages.\n276\t// For status code validation, the context includes the response type.\n277\tfunc getStatusCodeResponseContext(response interface{}) string {\n278\t\tswitch response.(type) {\n279\t\tcase *httptest.ResponseRecorder:\n280\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"httptest.ResponseRecorder\")\n281\t\tcase *http.Response:\n282\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"http.Response\")\n283\t\tdefault:\n284\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"unknown type: %T\", response)\n285\t\t}\n286\t}\n287\t\n288\t// formatCodeList formats a list of status codes with descriptions for error messages.\n289\t//\n290\t// This helper creates a readable string representation of multiple allowed codes,\n291\t// including their descriptions for better debugging.\n292\tfunc formatCodeList(codes []int) string {\n293\t\tvar parts []string\n294\t\tfor _, code := range codes {\n295\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, GetStatusCodeDescription(code)))\n296\t\t}\n297\t\treturn strings.Join(parts, \", \")\n298\t}\n299\t\n300\t// ValidateStatusCode validates that the HTTP response has the expected status code.\n301\t//\n302\t// This is the basic status code validation helper that checks if a response\n303\t// matches a single expected status code. It provides clear error messages\n304\t// indicating what was expected vs. what was received.\n305\t//\n306\t// Parameters:\n307\t//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n308\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (can be *httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n309\t//   - expectedCode: The expected HTTP status code (e.g., 200, 404, 500)\n310\t//\n311\t// Example:\n312\t//   ValidateStatusCode(t, w, 404)\n313\tfunc ValidateStatusCode(t *testing.T, response interface{}, expectedCode int) {\n314\t\tt.Helper()\n315\t\n316\t\tresult := AssertStatusCode(response, expectedCode, true)\n317\t\n318\t\tif !result.Match {\n319\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Status code validation failed:\\n  Expected: %d (%s)\\n  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n  Context:  %s\",\n320\t\t\t\tresult.Expected, result.ExpectedDescription,\n321\t\t\t\tresult.Actual, result.ActualDescription,\n322\t\t\t\tresult.ResponseContext)\n323\t\t}\n324\t}\n325\t\n326\t// ValidateStatusCodeAny validates that the HTTP response has one of the allowed status codes.\n327\t//\n328\t// This helper is useful when multiple status codes are acceptable for a given scenario.\n329\t// For example, when testing authentication, you might accept both 403 (Forbidden)\n330\t// and 401 (Unauthorized) depending on the authentication method.\n331\t//\n332\t// Parameters:\n333\t//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n334\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (can be *httptest.ResponseRecorder or *http.Response)\n335\t//   - allowedCodes: Slice of acceptable HTTP status codes\n336\t//\n337\t// Example:\n338\t//   ValidateStatusCodeAny(t, w, []int{200, 201, 204})\n339\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeAny(t *testing.T, response interface{}, allowedCodes []int) {\n340\t\tt.Helper()\n341\t\n342\t\tif len(allowedCodes) == 0 {\n343\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"ValidateStatusCodeAny: allowedCodes cannot be empty\")\n344\t\t\treturn\n345\t\t}\n346\t\n347\t\tresult := AssertStatusCodeAny(response, allowedCodes, true)\n348\t\n349\t\tif !result.Match {\n350\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Status code validation failed:\\n  Expected: one of [%s]\\n  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n  Context:  %s\",\n351\t\t\t\tresult.ExpectedDescription, result.Actual, result.ActualDescription, result.ResponseContext)\n352\t\t}\n353\t}\n354\t\n355\t// ValidateStatusCodePattern validates that an HTTP status code matches a pattern.\n356\t//\n357\t// This function validates status codes using pattern strings like '4xx', '5xx', etc.\n358\t// It extracts the century digit from the pattern and validates that the actual code\n359\t// falls within the corresponding range (e.g., '4xx' validates codes 400-499).\n360\t//\n361\t// Parameters:\n362\t//   - pattern: The status code pattern (e.g., '4xx', '5xx', '3xx', '2xx', '1xx')\n363\t//   - actual: The actual HTTP status code received\n364\t//\n365\t// Returns:\n366\t//   - nil if the status code matches the pattern\n367\t//   - error with descriptive message if pattern is invalid or code doesn't match\n368\t//\n369\t// Example (matching pattern):\n370\t//   err := ValidateStatusCodePattern('4xx', 404)\n371\t//   // err == nil\n372\t//\n373\t// Example (non-matching pattern):\n374\t//   err := ValidateStatusCodePattern('4xx', 200)\n375\t//   // err.Error() == \"status code 200 (OK) does not match pattern '4xx' (expected 400-499)\"\n376\t//\n377\t// Example (invalid pattern):\n378\t//   err := ValidateStatusCodePattern('invalid', 200)\n379\t//   // err.Error() == \"invalid status code pattern 'invalid': must be in format 'Nxx' where N is 1-5\"\n380\tfunc ValidateStatusCodePattern(pattern string, actual int) error {\n381\t\t// Parse the pattern to extract the century digit\n382\t\tcentury, err := parseStatusCodePattern(pattern)\n383\t\tif err != nil {\n384\t\t\treturn err\n385\t\t}\n386\t\n387\t\t// Calculate the expected range for this century\n388\t\tminCode := century * 100\n389\t\tmaxCode := minCode + 99\n390\t\n391\t\t// Validate the actual code falls within the range\n392\t\tif actual >= minCode && actual <= maxCode {\n393\t\t\treturn nil\n394\t\t}\n395\t\n396\t\t// Use the error formatting helper for enhanced range validation error messages\n397\t\t// This provides structured error information including:\n398\t\t// - Expected range (min/max)\n399\t\t// - Actual value received\n400\t\t// - Field name being validated\n401\t\t// - Contextual suggestions\n402\t\t// - Human-readable descriptions\n403\t\treturn validate.FormatStatusCodeRangeError(pattern, actual, \"\", \"status_code\")\n404\t}\n405\t\n406\t// parseStatusCodePattern parses a status code pattern like '4xx' and returns the century digit.\n407\t//\n408\t// This helper extracts the century digit from patterns in the format 'Nxx' where N is 1-5.\n409\t// It validates the pattern format and returns an error for invalid patterns.\n410\t//\n411\t// Parameters:\n412\t//   - pattern: The status code pattern to parse (e.g., '4xx', '5xx')\n413\t//\n414\t// Returns:\n415\t//   - The century digit (1-5) if the pattern is valid\n416\t//   - error if the pattern is invalid\n417\t//\n418\t// Example:\n419\t//   century, err := parseStatusCodePattern('4xx')\n420\t//   // century == 4, err == nil\n421\t//\n422\t// Example (invalid pattern):\n423\t//   century, err := parseStatusCodePattern('invalid')\n424\t//   // century == 0, err != nil\n425\tfunc parseStatusCodePattern(pattern string) (int, error) {\n426\t\tif len(pattern) != 3 {\n427\t\t\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid status code pattern '%s': must be in format 'Nxx' where N is 1-5\", pattern)\n428\t\t}\n429\t\n430\t\t// Check if pattern ends with 'xx'\n431\t\tif pattern[1] != 'x' || pattern[2] != 'x' {\n432\t\t\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid status code pattern '%s': must be in format 'Nxx' where N is 1-5\", pattern)\n433\t\t}\n434\t\n435\t\t// Extract the century digit\n436\t\tcentury := int(pattern[0] - '0')\n437\t\n438\t\t// Validate century is in valid HTTP range (1-5)\n439\t\tif century < 1 || century > 5 {\n440\t\t\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid status code pattern '%s': century digit must be 1-5\", pattern)\n441\t\t}\n442\t\n443\t\treturn century, nil\n444\t}\n445\t\n446\t// ValidateStatusCodeRange validates that the HTTP response status code falls within a range.\n447\t//\n448\t// This helper is useful for validating status codes by category, such as:\n449\t// - 2xx for success responses (200-299)\n450\t// - 4xx for client errors (400-499)\n451\t// - 5xx for server errors (500-599)\n452\t//\n453\t// Parameters:\n454\t//   - t: The testing instance for assertions\n455\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n456\t//   - minCode: Minimum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n457\t//   - maxCode: Maximum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n458\t//\n459\t// Example:\n460\t//   // Validate 2xx success response\n461\t//   ValidateStatusCodeRange(t, w, 200, 299)\n462\t//\n463\t//   // Validate 4xx client error\n464\t//   ValidateStatusCodeRange(t, w, 400, 499)\n465\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeRange(t *testing.T, response interface{}, minCode, maxCode int) {\n466\t\tt.Helper()\n467\t\n468\t\tif minCode > maxCode {\n469\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"ValidateStatusCodeRange: minCode (%d) cannot be greater than maxCode (%d)\", minCode, maxCode)\n470\t\t}\n471\t\n472\t\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n473\t\n474\t\tif actualCode < minCode || actualCode > maxCode {\n475\t\t\t// Use the error formatting helper for enhanced range validation error messages\n476\t\t\t// This provides structured error information including:\n477\t\t\t// - Expected range (min/max)\n478\t\t\t// - Actual value received\n479\t\t\t// - Field name being validated\n480\t\t\t// - Contextual suggestions\n481\t\t\t// - Human-readable descriptions\n482\t\t\tresponseContext := getStatusCodeResponseContext(response)\n483\t\t\tcontext := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation\", responseContext)\n484\t\t\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeMinRangeError(minCode, maxCode, actualCode, context, \"status_code\")\n485\t\n486\t\t\t// Output the enhanced error message\n487\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Status code validation failed:\\n%s\", err.Error())\n488\t\t}\n489\t}\n490\t\n491\t// CheckStatusCode checks if the HTTP response has the expected status code without asserting.\n492\t//\n493\t// This non-asserting version returns a boolean, allowing for conditional logic\n494\t// in tests. Useful when you need to handle different status codes programmatically\n495\t// rather than failing the test immediately.\n496\t//\n497\t// Parameters:\n498\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n499\t//   - expectedCode: The expected HTTP status code\n500\t//\n501\t// Returns:\n502\t//   - true if the response has the expected status code, false otherwise\n503\t//\n504\t// Example:\n505\t//   if CheckStatusCode(w, 404) {\n506\t//       // Handle 404 case\n507\t//   } else {\n508\t//       // Handle other cases\n509\t//   }\n510\tfunc CheckStatusCode(response interface{}, expectedCode int) bool {\n511\t\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n512\t\treturn actualCode == expectedCode\n513\t}\n514\t\n515\t// CheckStatusCodeAny checks if the HTTP response has one of the allowed status codes.\n516\t//\n517\t// This non-asserting version returns a boolean for flexible status code checking.\n518\t// Useful when you need to handle multiple acceptable status codes programmatically.\n519\t//\n520\t// Parameters:\n521\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n522\t//   - allowedCodes: Slice of acceptable HTTP status codes\n523\t//\n524\t// Returns:\n525\t//   - true if the response status code is in the allowed list, false otherwise\n526\t//\n527\t// Example:\n528\t//   allowedCodes := []int{200, 201, 204}\n529\t//   if CheckStatusCodeAny(w, allowedCodes) {\n530\t//       // Handle success case\n531\t//   } else {\n532\t//       // Handle error case\n533\t//   }\n534\tfunc CheckStatusCodeAny(response interface{}, allowedCodes []int) bool {\n535\t\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n536\t\n537\t\tfor _, allowedCode := range allowedCodes {\n538\t\t\tif actualCode == allowedCode {\n539\t\t\t\treturn true\n540\t\t\t}\n541\t\t}\n542\t\treturn false\n543\t}\n544\t\n545\t// CheckStatusCodeRange checks if the HTTP response status code falls within a range.\n546\t//\n547\t// This non-asserting version returns a boolean for range-based status code validation.\n548\t// Useful for category-based status code checking (e.g., 2xx, 4xx, 5xx).\n549\t//\n550\t// Parameters:\n551\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate\n552\t//   - minCode: Minimum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n553\t//   - maxCode: Maximum acceptable status code (inclusive)\n554\t//\n555\t// Returns:\n556\t//   - true if the status code is within the range, false otherwise\n557\t//\n558\t// Example:\n559\t//   // Check for any success code (2xx)\n560\t//   if CheckStatusCodeRange(w, 200, 299) {\n561\t//       // Handle success\n562\t//   }\n563\tfunc CheckStatusCodeRange(response interface{}, minCode, maxCode int) bool {\n564\t\tactualCode := getStatusCode(response)\n565\t\treturn actualCode >= minCode && actualCode <= maxCode\n566\t}\n567\t\n568\t// GetStatusCodeDescription returns a human-readable description for an HTTP status code.\n569\t//\n570\t// This helper provides descriptive names for common HTTP status codes, making\n571\t// test error messages more informative and easier to understand.\n572\t//\n573\t// Parameters:\n574\t//   - code: The HTTP status code\n575\t//\n576\t// Returns:\n577\t//   - A human-readable description string (e.g., \"Not Found\" for 404)\n578\t//\n579\t// Example:\n580\t//   desc := GetStatusCodeDescription(404)\n581\t//   // desc = \"Not Found\"\n582\tfunc GetStatusCodeDescription(code int) string {\n583\t\tdescriptions := map[int]string{\n584\t\t\t100: \"Continue\",\n585\t\t\t101: \"Switching Protocols\",\n586\t\t\t102: \"Processing\",\n587\t\t\t200: \"OK\",\n588\t\t\t201: \"Created\",\n589\t\t\t202: \"Accepted\",\n590\t\t\t203: \"Non-Authoritative Information\",\n591\t\t\t204: \"No Content\",\n592\t\t\t205: \"Reset Content\",\n593\t\t\t206: \"Partial Content\",\n594\t\t\t300: \"Multiple Choices\",\n595\t\t\t301: \"Moved Permanently\",\n596\t\t\t302: \"Found\",\n597\t\t\t303: \"See Other\",\n598\t\t\t304: \"Not Modified\",\n599\t\t\t305: \"Use Proxy\",\n600\t\t\t307: \"Temporary Redirect\",\n601\t\t\t308: \"Permanent Redirect\",\n602\t\t\t400: \"Bad Request\",\n603\t\t\t401: \"Unauthorized\",\n604\t\t\t402: \"Payment Required\",\n605\t\t\t403: \"Forbidden\",\n606\t\t\t404: \"Not Found\",\n607\t\t\t405: \"Method Not Allowed\",\n608\t\t\t406: \"Not Acceptable\",\n609\t\t\t407: \"Proxy Authentication Required\",\n610\t\t\t408: \"Request Timeout\",\n611\t\t\t409: \"Conflict\",\n612\t\t\t410: \"Gone\",\n613\t\t\t411: \"Length Required\",\n614\t\t\t412: \"Precondition Failed\",\n615\t\t\t413: \"Payload Too Large\",\n616\t\t\t414: \"URI Too Long\",\n617\t\t\t415: \"Unsupported Media Type\",\n618\t\t\t416: \"Range Not Satisfiable\",\n619\t\t\t417: \"Expectation Failed\",\n620\t\t\t418: \"I'm a teapot\",\n621\t\t\t422: \"Unprocessable Entity\",\n622\t\t\t423: \"Locked\",\n623\t\t\t424: \"Failed Dependency\",\n624\t\t\t426: \"Upgrade Required\",\n625\t\t\t428: \"Precondition Required\",\n626\t\t\t429: \"Too Many Requests\",\n627\t\t\t431: \"Request Header Fields Too Large\",\n628\t\t\t451: \"Unavailable For Legal Reasons\",\n629\t\t\t500: \"Internal Server HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(response, 204)\n28\tfunc HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(resp *http.Response, expected interface{}) bool {\n29\t\tif resp == nil {\n30\t\t\treturn false\n31\t\t}\n32\t\n33\t\tstatusCode := {\n41\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n42\t\t\t\t}\n43\t\t\t}\n44\t\t\treturn false\n45\t\tdefault:\n46\t\t\treturn false\n47\t\t}\n48\t}\n49\t\n50\t// HTTPStatusCodeIsError checks if an HTTP response's status code indicates an error (4xx or 5xx).\n51\t//\n52\t// Returns true if the response status code is in the 400-599 range, false otherwise.\n53\t//\n54\t// Example usage:\n55\t//\n56\t//\tif (4xx).\n68\t//\n69\t// Returns true if the response status code is in the 400-499 range, false otherwise.\n70\t//\n71\t// Example usage:\n72\t//\n73\t//\tif HTTPStatusCodeIsClientError(response) {\n74\t//\t    // Handle 4xx client error (bad request, unauthorized, etc.)\n75\t//\t}\n76\tfunc HTTPStatusCodeIsClientError(resp *http.Response) bool {\n77\t\tif resp == nil {\n78\t\t\treturn false\n79\t\t}\n80\t\n81\t\treturn resp.StatusCode >= 400 && resp.StatusCode < 500\n82\t}\n83\t\n84\t// HTTPStatusCodeIsServerError checks if an HTTP response's status code indicates a server error expected pattern.\n102\t// It supports pattern matching where the base content-type matches regardless of parameters.\n103\t// For example, \"application/json\" will match \"application/json; charset=utf-8\".\n104\t//\n105\t// Parameters:\n106\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n107\t//   - expected: The expected content-type pattern (e.g., \"application/json\")\n108\t//\n109\t// parseContentType(expected)\n130\t\n131\t\treturn actualMediaType == expectedMediaType\n132\t}\n133\t\n134\t// parseContentType extracts the base media type from a content-type string.\n135\t// It strips parameters like charset, boundary, etc.\n136\t// For example, \"application/json; charset=utf-8\" becomes \"application/json\".\n137\tfunc parseContentType(contentType string) string {\n138\t\tif contentType == \"\" {\n139\t\t\treturn \"\"\n140\t\t}\n141\t\n142\t\t// Split by semicolon to separate media type from parameters\n143\t\t// The first part before any semicolon is the {\n156\t\tstart := 0\n157\t\tend := len(s)\n158\t\n159\t\tfor start < end && (s[start] == ' ' || s[start] == '\\t' || s[start] == '\\n' || s[start] == '\\r') true\n943\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n944\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n945\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n946\t\t\t\t\t\tif !found {\n947\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected Issues to contain '%s', got: %v\", expectedIssue, context\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"100 not in 4xx range\",\n\t\t\tstatusRange: StatusCodeRange{\n\t\t\t\tMin:         400,\n\t\t\t\tMax:         499,\n\t\t\t\tDescription: \"Client Error\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tactual: 100,\n\t\t\tmustContain: []string{\n\t\t\t\t\"100\",\n\t\t\t\t\"400\",\n\t\t\t\t\"499\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Client Error\",\n\t\t\t\t\"300 codes below range\", // Distance information\n\t\t\t\t\"4xx\",                  // Range pattern context\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t// Create a response with {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected suggestions to be populated for validation type '%s'\", tt.validationType)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify error contains suggestions section\n\t\t\t// Status code causes:\")\n\t\t\tif !hasSuggestionsSection {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message to contain suggestions section, got: %s\", errMsg)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// verifies that response snippets\n// are properly truncated and sanitized in error messages.\nfunc TestValidationError_Content_ResponseSnippet(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname            string\n\t\tresponse        string\n\t\tsnippetContains string\n\t\tsnippetOmits    string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:            \"short response included fully\",\n\t\t\tresponse:        `{\"error\": `{\"error\": \"` + strings.Repeat(\"a\", 300) + `\"}`,\n\t\t\tsnippetContains: \"...\",\n\t\t\tsnippetOmits:    \"\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:            \"newlines are returns are sanitized\",\n\t\t\tresponse:        \"{\\r  \\\"error\\\": \\\"test\\\"\\r}\\r\",\n\t\t\tsnippetContains: \"{\",\n\t\t\tsnippetOmits:    \"\\r\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range == nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error for response: %s\", tt.response)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Convert error to string for content checking\n\t\t\terrMsg := err.Error()\n\n\t\t\t// Check for required content\n\t\t\tif tt.snippetContains != \"\" && !strings.Contains(errMsg, tt.snippetContains) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message to contain '%s', got: %s\", tt.snippetContains, errMsg)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Check that sanitized content is omitted\n\t\t\tif tt.snippetOmits != \"\" && strings.Contains(errMsg, tt.snippetOmits) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message to omit '%s', got: %s\", tt.snippetOmits, errMsg)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestValidationError_Content_DetailedResultFields verifies that all fields\n// in detailed validation results are correctly populated.\nfunc TestValidationError_Content_DetailedResultFields(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Create a response with status 404\n\tresp := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tresp.Code = 404\n\thttpResp := resp.Result()\n\n\t// Validate with multiple expected codes\n\texpectedCodes := []int{200, 201, 204}\n\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(httpResp, expectedCodes)\n\n\t// Verify all result fields are correctly populated\n\tif result.Valid {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected validation to fail\")\n\t}\n\n\tif result.ActualCode != 404 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected ActualCode to be 404, got %d\", result.ActualCode)\n\t}\n\n\tif len(result.ExpectedCodes) != len(expectedCodes) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected %d ExpectedCodes, got %d\", len(expectedCodes), len(result.ExpectedCodes))\n\t}\n\n\tfor i, code := range expectedCodes {\n\t\tif result.ExpectedCodes[i] != code {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected ExpectedCodes[%d] to be %d, got %d\", i, code, result.ExpectedCodes[i])\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif result.MatchedCode != nil {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected MatchedCode to be nil for failed validation, got %v\", *result.MatchedCode)\n\t}\n\n\tif result.MismatchDetails == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected MismatchDetails to be populated\")\n\t}\n\n\tif !result.IsClientError {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected IsClientError to be true for 404\")\n\t}\n\n\tif result.IsServerError {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected IsServerError to be false for 404\")\n\t}\n\n\tif result.Category != \"client_error\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected Category to be 'client_error', got '%s'\", result.Category)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestValidationError_Content_ErrorMessageValidationResult verifies that\n// error message validation results include all required fields.\nfunc TestValidationError_Content_ErrorMessageValidationResult(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname              string\n\t\tresponse          interface{}\n\t\tpattern           EnhancedErrorMessagePattern\n\t\twantValid         bool\n\t\twantFound         bool\n\t\twantPatternMatched bool\n\t\tmustContainIssues []string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"successful validation with pattern match\",\n\t\t\tresponse: map[string]interface{}{\"error\": \"invalid token\"},\n\t\t\tpattern: EnhancedErrorMessagePattern{\n\t\t\t\tPattern: \"invalid.*token\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantValid:         true,\n\t\t\twantFound:         true,\n\t\t\twantPatternMatched: true,\n\t\t\tmustContainIssues:  nil,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"failed true,\n\t\t\twantPatternMatched: true,\n\t\t\tmustContainIssues: []string{\n\t\t\t\t\"invalid\",\n\t\t\t\t\"token\",\n\t\t\t\t\"required\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"failed false,\n\t\t\twantFound:         true,\n\t\t\twantPatternMatched: true,\n\t\t\tmustContainIssues: []string{\n\t\t\t\t\"password\",\n\t\t\t\t\"forbidden\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := tt.wantValid {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected Valid to be %v, got %v\", tt.wantValid, result.Valid)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif result.Found != tt.wantFound {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected Found to be %v, got %v\", tt.wantFound, result.Found)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif result.PatternMatched != tt.wantPatternMatched {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected PatternMatched to be %v, got %v\", tt.wantPatternMatched, result.PatternMatched)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify issues contain expected content\n\t\t\tif tt.mustContainIssues != nil {\n\t\t\t\tif len(result.Issues) == 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected Issues to be populated for failed validation\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tfor _, expectedIssue := range tt.mustContainIssues {\n\t\t\t\t\tfound := false\n\t\t\t\t\tfor _, issue := range result.Issues {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif strings.Contains(issue, expectedIssue) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfound = true\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tif !found {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected Issues to contain '%s', got: %v\", expectedIssue, result.Issues)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// Example demonstrating how to use detailed validation error information\nfunc ExampleValidateStatusCodeWithDetails_errorContent() {\n\t// Create a response with status 404\n\tresp := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tresp.Code = 404\n\thttpResp := resp.Result()\n\n\t// Validate with expected status 200 (no context)\n\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(httpResp, 200)\n\n\t// Use detailed error information\n\tif !result.Valid {\n\t\tfmt.Printf(\"Validation failed:\\n%s\\n\", result.MismatchDetails)\n\t\tfmt.Printf(\"Category: %s\\n\", result.Category)\n\t}\n\n\t// Output:\n\t// Validation failed:\n\t// status_code validation failed\n\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n\t//   Received: 404 (Not Found)\n\t//   Range:    400-499 (Client Error)\n\t//   Details:\n\t//     - Status code category: 4xx - Client error responses\n\t//   Common causes:\n\t//     1. Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n\t//     2. Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n\t//     3. Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\n\t//     4. Review API versioning (URL path may have changed)\n\t//\n\t// Category: client_error\n}\n\n// ExampleValidateStatusCodeWithDetails_withContext demonstrates validation with request context\nfunc ExampleValidateStatusCodeWithDetails_withContext() {\n\t// Create a response with status 401\n\tresp := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tresp.Code = 401\n\thttpResp := resp.Result()\n\n\t// Validate with expected status 200 and provide request context\n\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(httpResp, 200, \"POST /api/users\")\n\n\t// Use detailed error information\n\tif !result.Valid {\n\t\tfmt.Printf(\"Validation failed:\\n%s\\n\", result.MismatchDetails)\n\t}\n\n\t// Output:\n\t// Validation failed:\n\t// status_code validation failed\n\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n\t//   Received: 401 (Unauthorized)\n\t//   Request:  POST /api/users\n\t//   Range:    400-499 (Client Error)\n\t//   Details:\n\t//     - Status code category: 4xx - Client error responses\n\t//   Common causes:\n\t//     1. Verify authentication credentials are correct\n\t//     2. Check if API token or session has expired\n\t//     3. Ensure Authorization header is properly formatted (e.g., 'Bearer <token>')\n\t//     4. Confirm authentication method is supported\n}\n\n// ExampleValidateStatusCodeWithDetails_rateLimit demonstrates 429 rate limit error with suggestions\nfunc ExampleValidateStatusCodeWithDetails_rateLimit() {\n\t// Create a response with status 429\n\tresp := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tresp.Code = 429\n\thttpResp := resp.Result()\n\n\t// Validate with expected status 200\n\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(httpResp, 200, \"GET /api/data\")\n\n\t// Use detailed error information\n\tif !result.Valid {\n\t\tfmt.Printf(\"Validation failed:\\n%s\\n\", result.MismatchDetails)\n\t}\n\n\t// Output:\n\t// Validation failed:\n\t// status_code validation failed\n\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n\t//   Received: 429 (Too Many Requests)\n\t//   Request:  GET /api/data\n\t//   Range:    400-499 (Client Error)\n\t//   Details:\n\t//     - Status code category: 4xx - Client error responses\n\t//   Common causes:\n\t//     1. Implement rate limiting and exponential backoff\n\t//     2. Check API quota limits and current usage\n\t//     3. Consider caching responses to reduce request frequency\n\t//     4. Review rate limit headers (Retry-After, X-RateLimit-Remaining)\n}\n\n// ExampleValidateStatusCodeWithDetails_serverError demonstrates 500 server error with suggestions\nfunc ExampleValidateStatusCodeWithDetails_serverError() {\n\t// Create a response with status 500\n\tresp := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tresp.Code = 500\n\thttpResp := resp.Result()\n\n\t// Validate with expected status 200\n\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(httpResp, 200, \"DELETE /api/resource/123\")\n\n\t// Use detailed error information\n\tif !result.Valid {\n\t\tfmt.Printf(\"Validation failed:\\n%s\\n\", result.MismatchDetails)\n\t\tfmt.Printf(\"Is server error: %v\\n\", result.IsServerError)\n\t}\n\n\t// Output:\n\t// Validation failed:\n\t// status_code validation failed\n\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n\t//   Received: 500 (Internal Server Error)\n\t//   Request:  DELETE /api/resource/123\n\t//   Range:    500-599 (Server Error)\n\t//   Details:\n\t//     - Status code category: 5xx - Server error responses\n\t//   Common causes:\n\t//     1. Implement retry logic with exponential backoff\n\t//     2. Check service status page for ongoing issues\n\t//     3. Contact support if the issue persists\n\t//     4. names and pattern details.\nfunc TestValidationError_Content_EnhancedErrorMessageValidation(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname           string\n\t\tresponse TestValidationError_Content_PatternMatchingDetails verifies that pattern\n// validation errors include detailed pattern information.\nfunc TestValidationError_Content_PatternMatchingDetails(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname           string\n\t\tresponse       string\n\t\texpectedPattern string\n\t\tisRegex        bool\n\t\tmustContain    []string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"regex pattern not matched with details\",\n\t\t\tresponse:       `{\"error\": \"access denied\"}`,\n\t\t\texpectedPattern: \"invalid.*token\",\n\t\t\tisRegex:        true,\n\t\t\tmustContain: []string{\n\t\t\t\t\"regex pattern 'invalid.*token'\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Field:\",\n\t\t\t\t\"error\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Pattern:\",\n\t\t\t\t\"regex pattern\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Details:\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Searched for regex pattern\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Actual message:\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"substring pattern not matched with details\",\n\t\t\tresponse:       `{\"message\": \"Authentication successful\"}`,\n\t\t\texpectedPattern: \"failed\",\n\t\t\tisRegex:        false,\n\t\t\tmustContain: []string{\n\t\t\t\t\"substring 'failed'\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Field:\",\n\t\t\t\t\"message\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Pattern:\",\n\t\t\t\t\"substring 'failed'\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Details:\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Searched for substring\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\terr := ValidateErrorMessage([]byte(tt.response), tt.expectedPattern)\n\n\t\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error for pattern '%s' with response: %s\", tt.expectedPattern, tt.response)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\terrMsg := err.Error()\n\n\t\t\t// Verify required content\n\t\t\tfor _, required := range tt.mustContain {\n\t\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(errMsg, required) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message to contain '%s'\\nGot: %s\", required, errMsg)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestValidationError_Content_MultipleMessageFields verifies that when\n// multiple error message fields are present, validation includes this Here are the key files and current error handling patterns:\n\n## Key Validation Files\n\n### Core Error Types and Structs\n- `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/validate/error_types.go` - Main ValidationError struct with comprehensive fields for error representation\n- `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/validate/error_type.go` - Generic ErrorType enum (required, format, range, etc.)\n- `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/validate/error_type_enum.go` - HTTP-specific ValidationErrorType enum\n- `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/validate/error_categories.go` - Error categorization system\n\n### Error Formatting and Messaging\n- `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/validate/error_formatting.go` - Core error formatting functions and helpers\n- `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/validate/format_helper.go` - ValidationFormatter builder pattern implementation\n- `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/validate/error_format_examples.go` - Examples of error formatting usage\n\n### Validation Logic\n- `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/validate/validate.go` - Main validation functions for status codes, messages, patterns\n- `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/error_status_validation.go` - HTTP status code validation utilities\n\n## Current Error Handling Patterns\n\n### 1. Error Types and Structs\n\nThe system uses a comprehensive `ValidationError` struct:\n\n```go\ntype ValidationError struct {\n    ErrorType    string   // Required: validation category\n    Message      string   // Required: human-readable description\n    Context      string   // Optional: where/when validation occurred\n    Expected     interface{} // Optional: expected value\n    Actual       interface{} // Optional: actual value received\n    FieldName    string   // Optional: specific field where error found\n    Location     string   // Optional: position information\n    RelatedFields []string // Optional: related fields for context\n    PatternDetails string // Optional: regex pattern matching failures\n    RangeInfo     string // Optional: range boundaries for validation\n    ValidationDetails []string // Optional: additional validation information\n    ResponseSnippet string // Optional: truncated response excerpt\n    Suggestions   []string // Optional: actionable recommendations\n}\n```\n\n### 2. Range Validation Logic\n\nThe system provides multiple approaches to range validation:\n\n**Pattern-based range validation:**\n- `ValidateStatusCodePattern(pattern string, actual int) error` - Validates against patterns like '4xx', '5xx'\n- `ValidateStatusCodeRange(resp, minCode, maxCode)` - Min/max range validation\n\n**Formatting functions:**\n- `FormatStatusCodeRangeError(pattern, actual, context, fieldName)` - Creates errors for pattern mismatches\n- `FormatStatusCodeMinRangeError(minValue, maxValue, actualValue, context, fieldName)` - Min/max range errors\n- `FormatErrorWithRange(fieldName, minValue, maxValue, actualValue)` - Generic range formatting\n\n### 3. Status Code Validation Logic\n\n**Validation functions:**\n- `HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(resp, expected)` - Basic status code validation\n- `ValidateStatusCodeInt(expected, actual)` - Integer comparison validation\n- `ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(resp, expected)` - Detailed validation with context\n- `AssertStatusCode(response, expectedCode, assertMode)` - Flexible assertion/validation\n\n**Error formatting:**\n- `FormatStatusCodeError(expected, actual, context)` - Status code mismatch errors\n- Enhanced error messages with status code descriptions (e.g., \"404 (Not Found)\")\n\n### 4. Message Pattern Validation Logic\n\n**Pattern matching functions:**\n- `ValidateErrorMessagePattern(bodyBytes, pattern, caseInsensitive)` - Regex pattern validation\n- `ValidateErrorMessagePatternWithConfig(bodyBytes, config)` - Configuration-based pattern validation\n- `ValidateErrorMessageWithDetails(responseBody, pattern)` - Enhanced pattern validation\n\n**Error formatting:**\n- `FormatErrorWithPattern(fieldName, pattern, actualValue)` - Pattern validation error formatting\n- `PatternDetails` field in ValidationError for pattern-specific information\n\n### 5. Error Formatting Patterns\n\n**Builder pattern:**\n```go\nerr := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n    WithExpected(200).\n    WithActual(404).\n    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n    Format()\n```\n\n**Convenience functions:**\n- `FormatStatusCodeError(expected, actual, context)`\n- `FormatStatusCodeRangeError(pattern, actual, context, fieldName)`\n- `FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName, expected, actual, customMessage)`\n- `FormatErrorWithRange(fieldName, minValue, maxValue, actualValue)`\n- `FormatErrorWithPattern(fieldName, pattern, actualValue)`\n\n## Error Type Categorization\n\nThe system organizes errors into categories:\n- **HTTP**: Status codes, content-type, headers\n- **Content**: Response body, structure, error messages\n- **Validation**: JSON schema, data validation, field validation\n- **Performance**: Timeouts, rate limits\n- **Security**: Authentication, authorization\n- **Custom**: Application-specific errors\n\n## Key Features\n\n1. **Comprehensive Error Information**: Each error includes expected/actual values, context, suggestions, and detailed diagnostics\n2. **Multiple Validation Modes**: Boolean checks, assertions, and detailed error reporting\n3. **Flexible Formatting**: Builder pattern, convenience functions, and custom formatting options\n4. **Type Safety**: Enum-based error types with validation\n5. **Human-Readable Output**: Detailed error messages with suggestions and common causes\n6. **Serialization Support**: JSON marshaling for all error structures\n\nThe ARMOR tt.actual\n\t\t\thttpResp := resp.Result()\n\n\t\t\t// Validate with details\n\t\t\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(httpResp, tt.expected)\n\n\t\t\t// Verify the result is invalid\n\t\t\tif result.Valid {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected validation to fail for status %d vs expected %v\", tt.actual, tt.expected)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify the mismatch details contain required information\n\t\t\tmismatchMsg := result.MismatchDetails\n\t\t\tif mismatchMsg == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected mismatch details to be populated, got empty string\")\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Check for required content\n\t\t\tfor _, required := range tt.mustContain {\n\t\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(mismatchMsg, required) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected mismatch details to contain '%s', got: %s\", required, mismatchMsg)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify all expected codes are stored\n\t\t\tif len(result.ExpectedCodes) != len(tt.expected) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected %d ExpectedCodes, got %d\", len(tt.expected), len(result.ExpectedCodes))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tfor i, code := range tt.expected {\n\t\t\t\tif result.ExpectedCodes[i] != code {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected ExpectedCodes[%d] to be %d, got %d\", i, code, result.ExpectedCodes[i])\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestValidationError_Content_RangeValidation verifies that range validation errors\n// include the range {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected suggestions to be populated for validation type '%s'\", tt.validationType)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify error contains suggestions section\n\t\t\t// Status code validation uses \"Common causes:\" while other types use \"Suggestions:\"\n\t\t\thasSuggestionsSection := strings.Contains(errMsg, \"Suggestions:\") || strings.Contains(errMsg, \"Common causes:\")\n\t\t\tif !hasSuggestionsSection {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message to contain suggestions section, got: %s\", errMsg)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n//
